mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year growth in industrial production signals real economic momentum. Higher INDPRO growth raises expectations for corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. This trend signal is always active, reflecting the supply-side health of the economy.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if over any rolling 3-year window the correlation between INDPRO annual change and 60-day forward returns is not positive (r < 0.0) or if the mean forward return following positive INDPRO growth is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy is highly restrictive, compressing credit availability and equity risk appetite. High FEDFUNDS levels directly increase discount rates for future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if during trigger-active periods the forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (mean < -1% and one-sided t-test p < 0.05) or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation combined with restrictive interest rates creates a stagflationary headwind for equities. When both CPIAUCSL annual inflation exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 5%, the joint effect of eroded real earnings and elevated discount rates dominates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint trigger does not yield a mean forward return below -1% over 60 days, or if the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, defined by consecutive rate hikes or elevated level persistence, further increases in FEDFUNDS amplify discount rate headwinds for equities. This mechanism is regime-conditional: only active when the Fed is actively tightening, not during neutral or easing periods.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if forward 20-day returns during active tightening episodes are not significantly negative (mean return less than -0.5% and one-sided t-test p < 0.10) or if a simple strategy of shorting SPX on trigger signals does not produce a positive Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings. Higher levels of RSAFS point to demand strength that feeds into near-term equity valuations. This always-on trend signal suggests positive forward returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if over any 2-year rolling window the correlation between RSAFS level and 5-day forward return is not positive (r <= 0.0) or if the mean forward return following high RSAFS (above median) is not significantly above zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly gains in nonfarm payrolls signal a robust labor market with rising income and consumer confidence. When PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000, the labor market is expanding at a healthy pace, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if during trigger-active months the forward 20-day return is not positive with at least 60% frequency, or if the mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and slowdown in payroll growth provides a powerful early recession signal. When UNRATE increases by at least 0.2 percentage points over three months while monthly PAYEMS growth falls below 150,000, labor market deterioration strongly predicts contracting economic activity and negative long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint trigger does not yield a mean 60-day forward return below -2% or if the interaction term in a bivariate probit model for negative returns is not significant at the 5% level over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery regime, strong quarterly GDP growth (above 4% annualized) validates the strength of the expansion. This regime-conditional signal indicates that economic momentum is self-reinforcing, driving corporate earnings higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth (annualized) > 4% and identified recovery regime
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if forward 60-day returns are not positive in at least 70% of recovery-triggered episodes, or if the average excess return over the risk-free rate is negative during such periods.
